channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Roblox debuts new child protection, parental control features
item.description: Following recent questions about child safety issues on the platform, Roblox announced Monday a number of changes designed to protect young users and increase parental control over the content they see.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: How does Elon Musk define ‘efficiency’? We asked his former employees
item.description: Elon Musk has big plans for his new Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) once Donald Trump takes the White House in January. Prior to the election, the Tesla CEO said he would help cut $2 trillion from the federal budget.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Nvidia rumored to be winding down production of RTX 4000 GPUs ahead of next-gen launches – which could mean some tempting Black Friday bargains
item.description: Nvidia is said to be transitioning to RTX 5000 graphics cards, with Lovelace models being discontinued – save for the RTX 4060.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Kyndryl, Microsoft expand mainframe modernization services
item.description: IT infrastructure services provider Kyndryl continues to build on its partnership with Microsoft, most recently adding new mainframe modernization support, security services, and cloud migration offerings.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: CIO Middle East and IDC hosts the inaugural CIO100 Awards, celebrating the region’s top 100 tech leaders
item.description: In a landmark event for the Middle East’s technology sector, Foundry and IDC have officially launched the inaugural CIO100 Middle East Awards in Dubai, spotlighting the region’s most influential and visionary CIOs and tech leaders.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Mobile AI opens new horizons for sustainable business growth in the digital age
item.description: As we navigate through 2024 and gear up for 2025, we are witnessing a pivotal moment in technological evolution where two transformative forces—5.5G (also referred to as 5G-Advanced or 5G-A) networks and artificial intelligence (AI)—are converging to reshape our digital landscape.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: New Chinese humanoid robot shows off its strength by lifting 35 pounds per hand
item.description: China's Shanghai Kepler Robotics has developed a new humanoid robot that can carry up to 35 pounds per hand for commercial applications across various industries
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AI chatbot reportedly says to eat deadly mushrooms
item.description: “FungiFriend” was added to a popular mushroom discussion Facebook group and encouraged members to “sauté in butter” a potentially dangerous mushroom.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Amazon developing driver eyeglasses to shave seconds off deliveries
item.description: Such directions could shave valuable seconds off each delivery by providing left or right directions off elevators and around obstacles such as gates or aggressive dogs.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: EU fines Meta over $840 million for ‘abusive practices’ favoring Facebook Marketplace
item.description: Meta received a fine of over $840 million on Wednesday for breaching the European Union’s antitrust rules as it relates to market domination after three years of legal proceedings.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Lawsuit alleging Elon Musk rigged Dogecoin is dropped
item.description: A lawsuit accusing Elon Musk of rigging Dogecoin is ending. Investors in the cryptocurrency who said the world’s richest person and his electric car company Tesla committed fraud and insider trading are withdrawing their appeal from an Aug. 29 dismissal of their case.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Exoskeleton helps paralyzed people regain independence
item.description: The WalkON Suit F1, an exoskeleton developed to help people with disabilities, can actually walk over to a person in a wheelchair, solving a major problem.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Getting started with Google Password Manager
item.description: If you’re still trying to remember all of your passwords and then type ’em into sites by hand, let me tell you: You’re doing it wrong.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Strategies to navigate the pitfalls of cloud costs
item.description: In my line of work, I often hear CIOs and CFOs say, “Cloud providers are bleeding us dry.” As ever-increasing cloud bills roll in, they’re demanding answers about what they’re paying for and why they’re paying so much. Public cloud providers are eager to oppose this line of questioning.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Chinese hackers target Tibetan websites in malware attack, cybersecurity group says
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A hacking group that is believed to be Chinese state-sponsored has compromised two websites with ties to the Tibetan community in an attack meant to install malware on users' computers, according to findings released Wednesday by a private cybersecurity firm.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Japan's SoftBank returns to profit after gains at Vision Fund and other investments
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology group SoftBank swung back to profitability in the July-September quarter, boosted by positive results in its Vision Fund investments. Tokyo-based SoftBank Group Corp.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, PIX 11
